Records.authors: Kälin, Gregor, Liu, Zhengwen, Porto, Rafael A.
Records.description: Cut two-loop integral family for the 3PM scattering angle in the EFT/PM approach. Defined in Eq. (master) as M^{(a,atilde)}_{n1 n2; i1...i5} = int dk1 dk2 delta(k1.u_a) delta(k2.u_atilde) / (A_{1,not a}^{n1} A_{2,not atilde}^{n2} D_1^{i1}...D_5^{i5}), with quadratic propagators D1=k1^2, D2=k2^2, D3=(k1+k2-q)^2, D4=(k1-q)^2, D5=(k2-q)^2 and linear (HQET-like) propagators A_{1,b}=k1.u_b, A_{2,b}=k2.u_b. The H-diagram in Fig. 1(g), with (a,atilde)=(1,2) and n1=n2=0, reduces via IBP (FIRE6, LiteRed) to a 7-element basis {I_{11111}, I_{11211}, I_{01101}, I_{11011}, I_{00211}, I_{00112}, I_{00111}} brought to canonical form (epsilon tool) in the variable x with gamma=(x^2+1)/(2x), with boundary conditions taken from the static (PN) limit Kite integrals. Diagrams (e), (f) vanish in D=4 after IBP. Two additional iteration integrals M^{(1,1)}_{11;11100} and M^{(1,2)}_{11;11100} are required and computed in the rest frame and via the canonical basis. Final 3PM impulse and scattering angle through O(G^3) given in Eqs. (dp3) and following.
Records.familyName: M^{(a,atilde)}_{n1 n2; i1...i5} (cut two-loop H family)
Records.location: Eq. (master) and surrounding text in 'Feynman Integrals' section; basis in Eq. (set); H diagram in Fig. 1(g)
